name: Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop
name_original: Molinos de la Marina Alta — circular por la Ruta Área Recreativa - Molins (verde oscuro)
description: This easy 2.7-mile (4.4 km) hiking loop in Parc Natural del Montgó features historical windmills and a picnic area with sea views.
region_intro: Hiking in El Montgó offers access to a distinctive limestone massif, rising over 750 meters above sea level between the coastal towns of Dénia and Jávea. The region is characterized by diverse natural features, including Mediterranean scrubland, regenerating pine forests, and impressive sea cliffs, particularly around Cape San Antonio. Its unique geological composition provides varied ecosystems and panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and surrounding coastlines. Best easy hiking trails in El Montgó The most popular easy hiking route is Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop, a 2.7 miles (4.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 9 minutes to complete, featuring historical windmills and sea views. Another top favourite among local hikers is Plana de Sant Jeroni loop from Reserva Natural dels Fons Marins del Cap de Sant Antoni, an easy 3.9 miles (6.2 km) path. This route offers views of the marine natural reserve and coastal landscapes. Local hikers also love the View of Montgó – View of Montgó loop from Xàbia / Jávea, a 3.2 miles (5.2 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 1 hour 21 minutes. Hiking in El Montgó is defined by its limestone massif, Mediterranean scrubland, and coastal paths, offering panoramic views. The network provides options for different ability levels, with a focus on easy hiking trails in this guide. The routes in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1200 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore El Montgó's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many easy hiking trails are available in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó offers over 20 easy hiking trails, providing a variety of experiences suitable for different preferences and fitness levels within the natural park.

Q: What natural features will I encounter on easy hikes in El Montgó?
A: On easy hikes in El Montgó, you can expect to see a distinctive limestone massif, Mediterranean scrubland, and regenerating pine forests. The trails often feature impressive sea cliffs, especially around Cape San Antonio, and offer panoramic views of the Mediterranean Sea and surrounding coastlines. The park is also home to diverse plant species and various birds of prey.

Q: Are there any circular easy hiking routes in El Montgó?
A: Yes, El Montgó features several easy circular routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a popular option, as is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/18942400" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Montgó – View of Montgó loop from Xàbia / Jávea&lt;/a&gt;, both offering scenic round trips.

Q: What interesting landmarks or points of interest can I see on easy trails in El Montgó?
A: Many easy trails in El Montgó lead to or pass by significant landmarks. You can explore historical windmills, such as those featured on the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/203776" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Windmills of Jávea&lt;/a&gt;, or visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/461688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cap de Sant Antoni Lighthouse&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views towards the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/416894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre del Gerro&lt;/a&gt;, an ancient watchtower.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about easy trails in El Montgó?
A: The easy trails in El Montgó are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 1200 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the stunning panoramic views of the Mediterranean, the well-maintained paths, and the unique blend of coastal and mountain scenery. The accessibility of these trails, combined with their natural beauty, makes them a favorite.

Q: Are the easy hiking trails in El Montgó suitable for families with children?
A: Yes, many easy hiking trails in El Montgó are suitable for families. These routes typically have gentle gradients and shorter distances, making them enjoyable for all ages. Trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1222917773/cape-san-antonio-windmills-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cape San Antonio Windmills Loop&lt;/a&gt; are particularly family-friendly, offering interesting sights without being overly strenuous.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the easy hiking trails in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó Natural Park generally welcomes dogs, but it's always recommended to keep them on a leash to protect the local wildlife and flora, especially given the park's rich biodiversity. Please ensure you clean up after your pet to maintain the natural environment for everyone.

Q: What is the best time of year to hike the easy trails in El Montgó?
A: The best time to hike the easy trails in El Montgó is typically during the spring (March to May) and autumn (September to November) months. During these periods, the weather is milder, making for more comfortable hiking conditions. Summers can be very hot, and while winters are generally mild, occasional rain may occur.

Q: Is there parking available near the easy hiking trails in El Montgó?
A: Yes, parking is generally available at various access points for the easy hiking trails in El Montgó, particularly near the starting points of popular routes in areas like Dénia and Jávea. Specific parking information can often be found on individual route descriptions.

Q: Are there any easy trails that offer views of the Cap de Sant Antoni Marine Natural Reserve?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38234033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Plana de Sant Jeroni loop from Reserva Natural dels Fons Marins del Cap de Sant Antoni&lt;/a&gt; is an easy path that provides excellent views of the marine natural reserve and the stunning coastal landscapes around Cap de Sant Antoni.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I see on an easy hike in El Montgó?
A: El Montgó is a protected natural park with rich biodiversity. On an easy hike, you might spot various bird species, including significant birds of prey like the Bonelli's eagle and peregrine falcons. The park is also home to a wide array of Mediterranean flora, with over 650 plant species, some of which are rare or endemic.

Q: Are there any easy trails that lead to or offer views of the Cova Tallada?
A: While the direct access to &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/68080" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cova Tallada&lt;/a&gt; is often described as a moderate hike due to some challenging sections, there are easy trails that offer views towards the coastal cliffs where the cave is located. For a less strenuous option, consider routes around the Cape San Antonio area which provide coastal vistas.
tips: great picnic spot with great views, shade and sun as you like

The remaining ruins of the former wheat mills still bear witness to the time when agriculture was of greater importance in the region. Some of them are now privately owned.
It is not known exactly when the mills were built. The last reference to the company probably dates back to 1911.

faqs.question: How long does it take to hike the Molinos de la Marina Alta circular route?
faqs.answer: This easy circular hike typically takes about 1 hour and 9 minutes to complete, making it a perfect option for a leisurely outing.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: Yes, the Molinos de la Marina Alta route is classified as easy and is suitable for all skill levels and individuals with any fitness level. Its accessible paths make it a great choice for beginners and families.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain is quite varied. You'll encounter approximately 4.01 km on streets, 1.96 km on gravel, and about 926 meters on unpaved sections. There are also natural paths, paved areas, and asphalt sections, offering a diverse walking experience.

faqs.question: What can I see along the Molinos de la Marina Alta trail?
faqs.answer: The route is centered around the historical Molinos (windmills) of Alta Marina. You'll also pass by the Windmills of Jávea and a Picnic area with sea view in Parc Natural del Montgó, offering scenic spots to rest and enjoy the surroundings.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other hiking trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route shares sections with several other official trails. A significant portion (83%) overlaps with El Camí IT5TR1 El Camí del Sud: de Dénia a Castelló de la Plana. You'll also find smaller sections that are part of Xàbia - Montgo, Camí Monastir, and Camí del Quartel.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Molinos de la Marina Alta trail?
faqs.answer: While specific parking details are not provided, the trail is described as starting from an 'Área Recreativa - Molins'. Look for designated parking areas near the recreational area or the windmills, which are typically available for visitors.

faqs.question: Is public transport available to reach the trailhead?
faqs.answer: Information on direct public transport to the specific trailhead is not readily available. It's advisable to check local bus schedules or ride-sharing options to Gata de Gorgos or nearby towns and then arrange for a short transfer to the recreational area.

faqs.question: Is the Molinos de la Marina Alta trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in natural areas like Parc Natural del Montgó are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep dogs on a leash to protect local wildlife and ensure a pleasant experience for all hikers. Please respect any posted signage regarding pets.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for this hike or Parc Natural del Montgó?
faqs.answer: No, typically there are no permits required or entrance fees to hike this trail or to access Parc Natural del Montgó. However, always check for any temporary local regulations or restrictions before your visit.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this route?
faqs.answer: The Molinos de la Marina Alta trail can be enjoyed year-round. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and lush scenery, while winter can be mild. Summer might be hot, so an early morning or late afternoon hike is recommended during those months.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like picnic areas or restrooms along the way?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route includes a Picnic area with sea view in Parc Natural del Montgó, which is a great spot to take a break and enjoy a meal. Information on restrooms is not specified, so it's wise to plan accordingly.

waypoints.name: Picnic area with sea view in Parc Natural del Montgó
